Media Object
Media Object base API
Transform C API Documentation

Introduction

The Transform C API extends the base Media C API with stream-processing capabilities. A transform_t handle embeds a media_t as its first member, inheriting all lifecycle, control, and stream operations. On top of this base the Transform API adds:

  • Stream processing – push data through the transform pipeline.

Because transform_t begins with a media_t, a pointer to a transform_t can always be safely cast to a media_t pointer, enabling polymorphic use.

Architecture Overview

A transform_t is a media element that processes data flowing from sink (input) streams to src (output) streams. It inherits media_t and adds stream processing.

Lifecycle

The transform follows the same state machine as the base media handle, without a Streaming state, as it is a passive element.

Full Lifecycle Sequence

The diagram below shows the complete interaction between the User (application), the Transform C public API functions, and the implementation callbacks during each state transition.

Note
Unlike the C++ Transform API, the C API does not have a Streaming state or auto-walk. Each transition must be called explicitly. Processing via transform_process_stream() is valid in the Prepared state.

Public API Reference

Lifecycle (inherited from Media C)

Function Description
transform_initialize() Transition from Null to Initialized.
transform_prepare() Transition from Initialized to Prepared.
transform_finalize() Transition from Prepared to Initialized.
transform_release() Transition from Initialized to Null.
transform_get_state() Query the current lifecycle state.

Information & Inspection (inherited from Media C)

Function Description
transform_about() Retrieve element metadata.
transform_get_version() Get the Transform C API version.
transform_inspect() Print a human-readable dump of the element.

Streams & Capabilities (inherited from Media C)

Function Description
transform_get_streams() List all input/output streams.
transform_get_stream_capabilities() Get current capabilities of a stream.
transform_set_stream_capabilities() Set desired capabilities on a stream.

Controls (inherited from Media C)

Function Description
transform_get_controls() List all available controls.
transform_get_control() Read the current value of a control.
transform_set_control() Write a new value to a control.

Transform-specific

Function Description
transform_process_stream() Push stream buffers through the transform.

Relationship to Media C

All transform_* wrapper functions delegate to the corresponding media_* function by casting the transform_t pointer to media_t. The transform-specific operation (process_stream) uses the additional virtual function pointers defined in transform_t.

Implementing a Transform Element

To build a concrete transform, create a transform_t whose first member is a fully-populated media_t (see Media C — Implementing a Media Element). Additionally, set the following transform-specific callback:

Function pointer Purpose
do_process_stream Receive input stream_buffers_t, process the data, and fill the output buffers. This is the core processing entry point.

Because a transform is a passive element with no Streaming state, the application drives data flow by calling transform_process_stream() explicitly. The implementation's do_process_stream callback is invoked each time.

Error Handling

Uses the same media_error_codes codes defined in error.h. Zero (MEDIA_ERROR_NONE) indicates success; negative values are errors.

Generated by  1.12.0
